title: Launching a Cloud Audit Log Platform for Healthcare Compliance

blocks.text: Led the 0→1 launch of a cloud audit logging platform that created a system of record for regulated user, medication, and system activity across enterprise healthcare environments. The platform improved audit readiness, strengthened customer trust in cloud adoption, and reduced modeled regulatory exposure by approximately $2M.
Problem
Audit data was fragmented across products and systems, making it difficult for healthcare IT, compliance, and internal teams to reconstruct regulated activity quickly and reliably.
Compliance teams relied on manual investigation to prepare for audits.
Enterprise customers needed stronger traceability before expanding cloud adoption.
Inconsistent audit data increased regulatory, operational, and customer trust risk.
Internal teams lacked a shared definition of what needed to be logged, retained, and made accessible.
My Role
As Product Manager, I owned product strategy, MVP scope, requirements, stakeholder alignment, and launch execution.
I partnered with Engineering, Security, Compliance, Legal, Customer Success, and enterprise stakeholders to define audit requirements, event structure, access controls, and launch priorities.
Solution

Built a centralized cloud audit logging platform that captured security-relevant and compliance-relevant activity across services and made those events searchable through secure, self-serve workflows.
Standardized audit event schema across services
Secure APIs for submitting audit events
Search and filtering for audit review workflows
Role-based access controls for sensitive audit data
Retention policies aligned with healthcare compliance needs
System-of-record architecture for regulated user and system activity
Execution / Product Decisions
Defined the MVP around the highest-risk regulated workflows instead of attempting to log every possible event on day one.
Partnered with Security and Compliance to define HIPAA-aligned audit requirements and evidence needs.
Worked with Engineering to define event schema, API contracts, retention logic, and access controls.
Validated audit review workflows with Customer Success and enterprise stakeholders.
Balanced compliance rigor with engineering feasibility to keep the platform scalable and launchable.
Created shared definitions for auditability, traceability, retention, and access across multiple product teams.
Tradeoffs & Product Decisions
Coverage vs launch speed: Prioritized the highest-risk MVP audit events first to deliver meaningful compliance value without blocking launch on long-tail event coverage.
Standardization vs team flexibility: Defined a shared event schema while allowing services to submit context-specific metadata.
Self-serve access vs security: Enabled searchable audit workflows while enforcing role-based access controls for sensitive data.
Compliance depth vs usability: Designed audit review around real investigation workflows so compliance teams could use the platform under time pressure.
Results & Impact
Achieved 100% traceability for defined security-relevant and compliance-relevant MVP events.
Reduced modeled regulatory exposure by approximately $2M.
Reduced audit preparation from weeks of manual investigation to minutes through self-serve audit access.
Deployed across 3 major enterprise health systems.
Improved enterprise readiness by giving customers stronger evidence of compliance, security, and operational control.
Created a scalable audit foundation for future cloud platform expansion.
Metrics Tracked
Audit event coverage for defined MVP workflows
Time to retrieve audit evidence
Number of systems/services submitting audit events
Number of enterprise deployments
Compliance and security review readiness
Customer adoption readiness for cloud workflows
Key Learnings
Strong platform products are often invisible when they work well. Their value comes from reducing risk, increasing trust, and giving teams a reliable foundation to move faster.

In regulated enterprise products, the product challenge is not just building the capability — it is defining the right system of record, aligning stakeholders on what 'complete' means, and making the workflow usable enough that teams rely on it.
The most important MVP decision was narrowing audit coverage to the highest-risk workflows first, then creating a scalable schema for future expansion.
Visual Diagrams
Audit Logging System Map — Product services → audit event API → centralized audit store → secure search interface → compliance/customer review.
Before vs After Audit Workflow — Before: manual investigation across fragmented logs. After: centralized search, filtering, and evidence retrieval.
Audit Event Schema — Event type | Actor | Action | Object | Timestamp | Source system | Metadata | Retention category
